items.title: 1 Safe-and-Steady Stock to Keep an Eye On and 2 We Question
items.description: Stability is great, but low-volatility stocks may struggle to deliver market-beating returns over time as they sometimes underperform during bull markets.

items.title: Amgen (AMGN): Buy, Sell, or Hold Post Q3 Earnings?
items.description: Amgen’s 17.6% return over the past six months has outpaced the S&P 500 by 6.3%, and its stock price has climbed to $328.43 per share. This was partly due to its solid quarterly results, and the performance may have investors wondering how to approach the situation.
